reviews.name: places/ChIJOS_IjZAXnIgRkANDlsQZpFs/reviews/Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2pWME5tbEZhREZQUlMxVFJFNU5PSHAxVEU5bFVWRRAB
reviews.text:
9/18/2025

Seriously disappointed in Forever Young. I have been a client since mid March 2025. During this period of time I have only seen a nurse practitioner once and my care has been horrible. I have been really offered no path to proper TRT levels and given very little understanding of what my actual levels should be. After conducting the first labs I was given TRT and told to take 50 units…a month went by and no mention of when I would need to do labs again to monitor my levels. I had to call in and ask out of concern that my levels might be too high. In July I did my labs and sure enough…my levels were over 1500. Of course I had to request a meeting to try to understand what needed to be done. I was told to go give blood. I was not given any clear indication of what else could be done to get my levels to a proper dosage. The only direction I was given was I needed to decrease to 45 units. I told the receptionist I would like to speak with the owner. Her response was he isn’t here. I asked to send a message that I would like to peak with him about what is needed to get me levels correct. He never contacted me. Now it is September and I was finally scheduled to do labs again. Labs were completed and I had to wait to speak to someone…So now I’m speaking with someone over the phone (receptionist) and she tells me my levels are 1450 test (high) and 1100 estrogen (high)…I’m told I can either wait till next month and they would send me additional estrogen blockers or they could write me script and I can go pay for it. Seriously!?  I asked to speak with the manager and was told that she’s busy…even after she had just put me on hold to speak with the office manager.

At this point my main concern is the potential for the bad side effects of having high test levels. The main reason why I went to this facility in the first place was, I believed the owners words in his recent podcast and thought that I would be receiving individual care. My experience has been quite the contrary…I will be seeking care elsewhere. I almost never do reviews, but given the health nature and the potential ramifications to my health, I believe it’s important to do this review. After over six months of TRT therapy I’ve only spoke to a nurse practitioner twice (once over the phone and once in person). And at no point of time did the owner reach out to me even after I requested that the he contact me for a consult.

9/27/2025 - UPDATE:
After speaking with the receptionist on 9/18/25 and explaining my issue with Forever Young, I decided to work TST needs through the VA.  I let the receptionist know that I was going to discontinue my membership with Forever Young.

On 9/25/2025 I went to Forever Young to discontinue my membership and have my record forwarded to the VA.

Today I received a mailer dated 22 September, 2025 (see attached mailer) stating that I am being "DISMISSED" from care at Forever Young.  Apparently the owner read my Google review and his response is to send me a auto-signed mailer.  I was dismissed for "INAPPROPRIATE BEHAVIOR THAT HAS COMPROMISED THE PROVIDER-PATIENT RELATIONSHIP."  I guess writing an honest assessment of the handling of my care since April is considered inappropriate.

I'm not surprised that Forever Young couldn't even get my name correct on this mailer.  My last name is Andersen not Scott.  Scott is my middle name.

reviews.name: places/ChIJOS_IjZAXnIgRkANDlsQZpFs/reviews/Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2xsWWFtaHJZUzFSVGs5alVXNVZUV3RaVmpWbVdsRRAB
reviews.text:
I was eager to work with Forever Young! However, the time frame from calls, to appts, results, and prescriptions is very disappointing. I received my lab results on a Tuesday (over the phone and was read the results). She mentioned that I would receive an email about which subscription I’d like to move forward with and that I’d receive my prescription in 7-10 days. I thought that seemed a little long but decided to proceed forward. They weren’t even going to send me a copy of my lab results (she said they don’t typically send those to patients). But ended up emailing them to me bc I was persistent.
A week later I get an email stating that my subscription was received. So it took them another week to process!!  Annddd another week to wait. I finally decide to call them. After hours of calling with no answer I finally get someone on the phone and told that pharmacy is on back order for my injections. Call the pharmacy and (which is not local) told that they had “just received the shipment” and it would be a few days for processing. I wanted another 7 business days. Still nothing. No option to change to a compound or use a local pharmacy. This is nothing more than a money grab and wasted nearly two months of my time without receiving a single product.  I ended up cancelling my subscription and asked for a refund for my subscription. Very disappointed. Again, another money grab.

Updated review
Previously I rateted them at 5 stars. However today 11-4 I called and the lady who normally checks me out after the appointment was incredibly rude. I was trying to explain how I couldn't fill my medicine on this base because the government shut down has made it where they cant order meds. I needed it sent to keesler or another pharmacy... she said they wouldn't do that and that she didn't know what to tell me and then she HUNG UP on me. I get people have off days, but good grief maybe she shouldn't answer phones. This is medication I need. It was prescribed from their office, not sure why my request was so problematic. One bad experience can ruin a good thing. Guess I just dont get treated till this shutdown is over!

Previous review
I see NP Ashley House and she has been wonderfull my A1C has gone from 11 to 5 in less than six months. She has listened to me and made changes to my medicine to actually help me. Dara in the lab is probably the kindest woman I've ever met and so very good at what she does. The lady's in the office no matter how busy they have been, have been kind to me every time I've encountered them. Nurse Debbie is the best. She is so helpful and returns calls quickly, she follows through and that means alot!

reviews.text: My 18 year old daughter who is still in highschool made her appointment bc we are showing her how to be an adult. The day of her appointment , her brother also had an appointment. His appointment ran a little over so she did the adult thing and called and let them know what was going on. They wouldn’t see her that day which I understand but she has called them multiple times to reschedule and they tell her to call back later. Finally she talks to them today and they tell her the doctor won’t see her bc she was a no call no show!!! Your office is and has been extremely hard to even get in contact with and to continue to tell a patient to call back to make an appointment later is unprofessional! I am disgusted with your services and can not recommend you to anyone! What’s sad is my daughter really needs to be seen and to be turned away is so sad.

reviews.name: places/ChIJObu7xj09nIgRJpQaW9Z2Xo4/reviews/ChdDSUhNMG9nS0VJQ0FnSURmcVliX3hRRRAB
reviews.text:
Unacceptable care. I hope that the dangerous and uncaring level of communication is a result overscheduling, overworking, or poor management and not outright planned malfeasance. The front staff (who really do seem overworked. I do not place blame on them) can only leave messages to the doctors and nurses on staff. The doctors and nurses on staff will never call you, even if you ask for a callback and the front staff tells you they will. Even if you have a question about your medicine, even if it affects your health. The communication goes one way, and you'll never know if they even got the messages. If there's an issue with your insurance, they will not do anything by themselves to communicate with the insurance even if it's a simple issue that can be cleared up with a single phone call, email, or fax. It's up to you to guide their hand and force them along every step of the process. This will take weeks, and you'll run out of your medication.

I know dealing with insurance requires too much time for medical staff. That's not the issue I have. It's all broken promises from underinformed people at the front line. I never once got a callback despite assurances that it was going to happen.

Maybe I'm not as high a priority as other patients. I can understand that. There are people out there who are more sick than me. But they never discussed that with me (once again, they're impossible to get into contact with) and I'd like to be part of that decision.

Don't trust these people with your life.

reviews.text: This health program has truly been life-changing for me. Allison Smith takes the time to listen, asks thoughtful questions, and looks at the whole picture instead of just symptoms. I’ve never felt rushed or dismissed. Her functional medicine approach helped me understand what was actually going on in my body and gave me a clear, realistic plan to heal. I finally feel hopeful and supported. If you’re looking for a doctor who genuinely cares and gets to the root cause, Dr. Smith is the one. Highly recommend!

reviews.name: places/ChIJaSnnqTbRKYYRfbNtHE_hIYs/reviews/Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2xKdlEzYzVTMUZWUTNkYVRtNWlibGhpUTA5SE9VRRAB
reviews.text: I had a great experience working with Allison! She is incredibly knowledgeable and cares about her clients. She made me feel comfortable, heard, and supported, and I ended up feeling so much better overall. I would highly recommend her to anyone looking for someone who is both skilled and compassionate — you will be in great hands!

reviews.name: places/ChIJaSnnqTbRKYYRfbNtHE_hIYs/reviews/Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT214aU0zbDBjV2h1TVVSUFFWbHFNRFJqT1ZOb09FRRAB
reviews.text:
I cannot say enough wonderful things about Allison! When I first came to her, I was struggling with severe fatigue, brain fog, and a number of other frustrating symptoms. She immediately took the time to truly listen and ordered detailed bloodwork to get to the root of the issue. What amazed me most was how she took my standard results and turned them into pages of clear, personalized explanations that helped me understand why I was feeling the way I was.

Allison created a customized plan for me that included targeted supplements and practical lifestyle recommendations, and the results have been life-changing. I feel like myself again!

She is incredibly easy to work with and always available when you need her. I especially love that when I have a question, she often responds with a personalized video explaining everything in detail—it’s such a thoughtful touch that shows how much she cares. Allison truly pours her heart into her work, and it’s clear that helping people feel their best is her passion.

I’ve recommended her to my friends and family, and I’m so grateful to have found her. She’s the absolute best!

reviews.name: places/ChIJaSnnqTbRKYYRfbNtHE_hIYs/reviews/Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2xFdE16TnhVRTlXYjBseFdVVlNhazF6T0hwSVZrRRAB
reviews.text: After pregnancies, years of nursing and a miscarriage, I noticed that things felt off and knew I had some nutrient gaps. I found Allison and she has been so helpful in restoring my health! She starts with the basic foundations and gave helpful tips for me and my own needs. Then through running extensive bloodwork, she was able to find and piece together a plan to help my body function better. She has been so helpful at answering all of my questions along the way. I’m so thankful for Allison and Peak!

reviews.name: places/ChIJaSnnqTbRKYYRfbNtHE_hIYs/reviews/Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2xkM2FYTkZPSFJRZFc5dFZsQjRkR1pSUTI0d1VuYxAB

reviews.text: I have been a member of the Men’s Clinic for just over 2 years now.  After feeling like a had lost some of my “get-up” in my day to day and in the gym, I was referred by a friend to visit The Men’s Clinic. reviews.name: places/ChIJDYnQ893RKYYRGJz6VNx6ASc/reviews/ChdDSUhNMG9nS0VJQ0FnSURXcGJ5RjV3RRAB
reviews.text:
I was in some bad shape entering the men's clinic. Being released from hospital nearly dieing from Pulmonary embolism (Blood Clots) 4 large clots in my lungs. I made a vow to God, if he will get me through this one, I will do better with my health.

I was greeted and felt welcomed from the start. Having medical staff walk me through everything and helping reach my goals. I am forever grateful to The Men's Clinic.  Dr. Adams told me "we all have a dash between our born date and our end date. The Men's Clinic helps that dash be alot better and healthier." reviews.text: First, I had some blood work done to check some things and they used the wrong diagnostic code when billing the insurance, causing the insurance company to deny payment. That cost me $531 out of pocket. The insurance company said that they could submit a amended bill with the correct diagnosis and it would be paid, they refused stating that the diagnosis was a legal document and could not be changed although it was wrong. From this point on, anything to do with that issue will not be covered with my insurance and I will have to get another doctor for that issue. I was prescribed Synthroid and was allowed the generic version, but that was changed by them so that the generic was not allowed. When I asked about it, they said that the generic was no longer available. My pharmacist said that not only was it available, but there was not even talk about it becoming unavailable. When this was brought to my caregiver's attention they disputed the availability and really wanted me to get the name brand stuff. I had to talk them into giving me a generic so I could afford it, and instead of just authorizing the generic they changed the prescription to something totally different. The pharmacist offered to call and explain that what I was being told was not true, but I let it lie. Now, my diabetic meds have been out for almost three weeks and after repeated calls and left messages (from me and the pharmacy), they will not give one month's refill to last me until my next appointment. They won't even return a phone call.

reviews.name: places/ChIJvThjUASJf4gRaTe6VCa_hdQ/reviews/Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2xCTVVUZDVObTFsUlV0WFh6ZDZVVW8zU1VGV2RtYxAB
reviews.text:
I really try not to post negative reviews. EVER. I think there’s already enough negativity in the world. But I have genuinely never had an experience like this. If you are looking for a doctor’s office, STEER CLEAR of Midsouth Integrative Medical. From the very first time I went, things have felt off, but I tried to push it off as just anxiety.
We switched to Midsouth Integrative Medical when my youngest daughter was born. Last January, I took my oldest daughter there to be tested for Flu and Strep. They had us pull to the back, came to the car and took her blood pressure, nurse came out and spoke to me like I was basically dumb for bringing her for a fever. That was the extent of the visit. I thought it was odd, but again I was in the thick of postpartum anxiety and thought I was just being crazy. After the experience with the nurse, I shared with their office that I was very dissatisfied with the treatment we received at our visit and told them that they didn’t even take my daughter’s vitals— not even her temperature when I brought her in for a FEVER. Since they made the mistake and didn’t take her vitals, I had to take her BACK up there same day for their mistake. This same scenario has also happened to my sister in law and she has had to take her child back up there because they failed to take vitals.
Fast forward to the summer, I took my youngest up there for a sick visit. Same thing, pulled up, they came to the car, they actually took her vitals this time. Great. Then, they told me they were going to charge me an extra $50 on top of my copay to “put on hold” on her account “in case I ever cancel an appointment in the future.” I have never had a doctor’s office require this especially when I’ve never cancelled an appointment. Immediately I was skeptical.
Then, in January of this year, I received a $25 bill for my oldest— which I found odd because she has not been back there since last January because I switched her to another office. I called and asked what it was for since it had been a year since she was even seen in office. At first, they couldn’t even find her in their system because they had her BIRTHDAY WRONG! IN HER MEDICAL RECORD! They said I did not pay my copay at the January 2025 appointment. Odd that they’re just now finding this. I told them that I had recently switched my children’s doctor, and they can just use the weird $50 hold to pay the copay. She said that would be fine. I thought the problem was solved.
Today, I get ANOTHER $25 for my oldest for the same appointment day— which I can only assume is the copay for the SECOND appointment that day when I had to come BACK UP THERE because they failed to do their job correctly. I can only assume that because I have called all day long and keep getting a voicemail.
Again, I normally never speak negatively about businesses, but I am so beyond aggravated at this situation. So, if you are looking for a medical provider, I would NOT suggest Midsouth Integrative Medical.

reviews.text:
Office staff and billing is incredibly incompetent. After 12 months of billing issues and screw ups on their end (on the medical and chiropractic side), I decided to just let it all go, even though they owed us money. My EOBs NEVER matched what they were charging me at each appointment AND the front desk and billing lady would reassure me multiple times that she would check on things and get it sorted out, but never did, despite me going in the office multiple times to follow up. HOWEVER in January, I felt trapped (not having found another pediatrician yet) and needed to schedule my newborn for an appointment to check his bilirubin. I specifically stated this is the only reason for the appointment. When we show up for the appointment, Molly comes in and proceeds to tell us that the front desk should never have made that appointment for us because they do not have the supplies or equipment to draw the blood for the testing. "We don't do that here and you coming in today was a complete waste of your time." That's fine; I understand that although they boast in newborn care, they do not have enough patients to justify keeping certain supplies on hand. So, after having been told by the provider herself that we wasted our time and the appointment was made by mistake and she couldn't help us that day, they still proceeded to not only charge my insurance, but also charge us a copay 8 months later!
I had my entire family as patients at this practice and nothing ever went smoothly or even routinely for us. There were many other issues with diagnosing things for my other baby, but again, I have tried to move past this and have successfully found another provider. So, this bad review is for the office staff!
Poor experience OVERALL though!
